D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

Viridis vs Jet

why the old rainbow lies — jet's lightness zigzags and invents bands, while viridis climbs smoothly

A good data colormap must rise monotonically in perceived lightness, so equal data steps look equally far apart. Jet — the old rainbow — peaks bright in the middle and darkens at both ends, fabricating boundaries that aren't in the data. Viridis was built to climb in lightness all the way.

Compare the two ramps and, below each, its perceived-lightness curve. Convert to greyscale and jet collapses into a bright stripe — a feature that exists only in the colour. Slide the marker to read both at one data value.
